name: Maji Solutions
peopleReachedDefinition: Direct count of unique individuals registered at Maji water points who gained new access to clean water. Equals the 'Individuals Gaining Water Access' output indicator — a direct 1:1 mapping with no multiplier applied.
householdsReachedDefinition: Estimated as people reached (26,400) / average household size of 4.5 persons = 5,867, based on Kenya DHS 2022 data. Represents unique households gaining access to clean water.
jobsCreatedDefinition: Full-time equivalent (FTE) jobs directly created by Maji Solutions: 25 installation and plumbing technicians, 18 maintenance and operations staff, 12 community outreach workers. Verified through payroll records.

outputs.label: WASH Facilities Installed
outputs.fullName: Water, Sanitation & Hygiene Facilities Installed (IRIS+ PD3668)
outputs.methodology: Cumulative count of commissioned water kiosks, boreholes, and sanitation blocks since inception
outputs.source: Commissioning reports and GPS-verified field audits

outputs.label: Individuals Gaining Water Access
outputs.fullName: Individuals Gaining New Access to Clean Water (IRIS+ PI4060)
outputs.methodology: Unique individuals registered at water points via smart card system, counted at first access event

outputs.label: Service Hours of Water
outputs.fullName: Daily Hours of Clean Water Service Provided (IRIS+ PI5683)
outputs.methodology: Sum of operational hours per day across all active facilities, measured via IoT flow sensors
outputs.source: IoT monitoring platform, daily average over last reporting period

outputs.label: Households Reached
outputs.fullName: Number of unique households with new or improved water and sanitation access
outputs.methodology: Derived from individuals with water access divided by average household size of 4.5 persons (Kenya census data)
outputs.source: Project registration data and Kenya census

outputs.label: Jobs Created
outputs.fullName: Direct full-time equivalent jobs created through Maji Solutions operations
outputs.methodology: Count of full-time equivalent positions, verified quarterly through payroll records

outcomes.label: Waterborne Disease Reduction
outcomes.fullName: Reduction in Waterborne Disease Incidence in Service Areas
outcomes.methodology: Compared health clinic admission data for waterborne diseases in Maji service areas vs matched control areas without Maji facilities
outcomes.source: County health facility records, semi-annual analysis

outcomes.label: Time Saved in Water Collection
outcomes.fullName: Average Daily Time Saved on Water Collection per Household
outcomes.methodology: Household time-use surveys at baseline (pre-installation) and endline, sample of 300 households per county

outcomes.label: Water Quality Standards Met
outcomes.fullName: Percentage of Facilities Meeting WHO Water Quality Standards
outcomes.methodology: Quarterly water quality testing against WHO Guidelines for Drinking-water Quality (turbidity, E. coli, pH, chlorine residual)

outcomes.label: Women Beneficiary %
outcomes.fullName: Percentage of total beneficiaries who are female
outcomes.methodology: Calculated from sex-disaggregated beneficiary registration data

financials.label: Revenue
financials.fullName: Semi-annual gross revenue from water service fees, installation charges, and maintenance contracts
financials.methodology: Gross revenue on accrual basis per IFRS 15. Includes water service fees from pay-per-use smart card transactions, installation charges for new water points, and recurring maintenance contracts with community operators.
financials.source: Audited financial statements and management accounts

financials.label: Valuation
financials.fullName: Fair market value of the fund's equity position
financials.methodology: Fair market value via DCF and comparable transactions.
financials.source: Independent valuation report and management estimate

financials.label: Capital Deployed
financials.fullName: Total committed capital called and deployed

profile.description: Maji Solutions designs, installs, and maintains decentralized water treatment and sanitation systems serving peri-urban and rural communities across Kenya. Operating in Nairobi County, Mombasa County, and Kisumu County, the company partners with local entrepreneurs through a franchise model to manage water kiosks and sanitation service points.
profile.theoryOfChange: By providing reliable access to safe, affordable drinking water through community-level water treatment systems, Maji reduces waterborne disease incidence, decreases time spent collecting water (especially for women and girls), and improves overall household health and economic productivity. Clean water access enables school attendance and reduces healthcare expenditure.
profile.targetBeneficiaries: Low-income peri-urban and rural communities lacking reliable piped water access in Nairobi County, Mombasa County, and Kisumu County, with particular focus on women and children who bear the primary burden of water collection.
profile.geographicFocus: Nairobi County, Mombasa County, and Kisumu County, Kenya. 38 operational water points across 3 counties.
profile.implementationApproach: Franchise model: Maji designs and installs water treatment systems in coordination with county water service boards under Kenya's Water Act, then partners with local entrepreneurs to operate water kiosks. Revenue is collected via M-Pesa mobile money. IoT sensors monitor water quality and system performance remotely.
profile.reportingFrequency: Semi-annual (H1/H2), with monthly water quality testing reports shared with regional health authorities and quarterly impact updates to investors.
